name: Motivosity
description: Motivosity is an employee engagement platform that helps develop your managers and build great cultures. Learn more about Motivosity

reviews.review_title: I had my doubts at first, but Motivosity has had a meaningful impact at my company.
reviews.review_description: When I heard from our HR team that we'd be rolling out Motivosity, I had some serious doubts about it. The idea of thanking coworkers with a $1 "tip" here and there seemed gimmicky at best and borderline insulting at worse. However, after using it for over 2 years, I'm happy to admit I was wrong. They've somehow built a platform that doesn't feel weird to use at all, and actually succeeds in remind you to give thanks. (My own rewards have basically funded my casual video game habit as well.)
reviews.review_pros: Motivosity helps remind me to pause and take time to thank and recognize people on my team (and other teams) for going above and beyond. The rewards system is a clever way of gamifying recognition too.
reviews.review_cons: I'd have to get very nit-picky to identify any true cons with the platform, but I do wish there were more creative options for attachments when making a post. You're essentially limited to the stock GIF search it provides, which is typically lacking in options. You can bring in your own file attachments instead, but that's extra work for something that should be quick.

reviews.review_title: Motivosity- get if you want, or look into Awardco instead
reviews.review_description: Our overall experience with motivosity's platform and services is 4/5 stars. 3.85/5 stars if I were to be picky.
reviews.review_pros: Its easy to use and employees love it! What a great tool in helping to drive our recognition and appreciation culture.
reviews.review_cons: Over our time with Motivosity, we have encountered a multitude of bugs, errors, and broken processes their development teams can't explain. Customer service response times are also very lengthy. We even have a dedicated relationship manager that is mostly helpful most of the time.

reviews.review_title: Easy to Use, But Room for Improvement
reviews.review_description: The platform is generally easy to navigate and user-friendly, which I appreciate. However, I believe there’s room for improvement in a few areas that could enhance the overall experience. Streamlining certain features and addressing minor usability issues would make it even more effective
reviews.review_pros: Writing an appreciation is simple, but meaningful. Giving Rezbucks to those who truly deserve it is more than just a thank-you—it’s a way to recognize the effort, attitude, and impact they bring to the team. It’s a small gesture that goes a long way in showing people they’re seen and valued.
reviews.review_cons: The filter settings keep resetting, causing me to lose the feed of my team. It’s frustrating to have to manually reapply the filter every time I log in. Additionally, I’d really appreciate email notifications when someone comments on or likes a post. Currently, I have to log into Motivosity and search for the post just to see if there’s been any activity, which feels inefficient. These improvements would make the platform much more user-friendly and help ensure better engagement across the team.

reviews.review_description: Overall, hands down one of the best platforms I have used for a company.
reviews.review_pros: The little characters and ratings to see what type of employee you are.
reviews.review_cons: There should be more options for examples on why we are being thanked.

reviews.author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
reviews.review_description: overall i like motivosity. theres just so many out there these days, and reviewing i feel led to share my experience. i used to reward my staff when they reached a team goal or accomplished a major task as a star my only downfall thus far would be the layout of the desktop version on my laptop. if its the same else where id have to decline continuing this service. just make it easier for us to navigate through
reviews.review_pros: dashboard, being able to track which employee got what goal accomplished, the leaderboard is also overall one of the best ive saw yet
reviews.review_cons: need to see a more easy interface and layout, and maybe more local vendors to provide incentives for.

reviews.review_pros: The easiness as a manager/leader to review and filter while appreciating staff
reviews.review_cons: compared to other platforms I cant say there is anything I dont like about Motivosity.

reviews.review_title: Motivosity is a great way to recognize your peers.
reviews.review_description: I love the reminders, the ability to recognize anyone in my organization and the data it provides when I review who I recognized and how long ago. I love it!
reviews.review_pros: I absolutely love the ability to recognize my peers across a very large matrix organization using Motivosity.
reviews.review_cons: Initially, it was challenging to understand each function because I am not an intuitive tech user, but I was able to figure it out quickly.

reviews.review_description: We have been using motiviosity for at least 5 years now. Every year we evaluate if we will continue and the answer is always yes. It allows that team and individual recognition by all staff, not just managers. Helps our staff to remain connected to other departments and their efforts/accomplishments
reviews.review_pros: Ease of use is probably the best feature of motivosity. It allows all our employees to participate in team recognition.
reviews.review_cons: I wish I could give motivosity bucks when commenting on a post privately. From an admin side it is hard, or used to be, to reset the funding password to make changes to the funding account.

reviews.review_title: Great place to post positive comments for our team members
reviews.review_description: I really enjoy thanking my co-workers and just seeing the accomplishments posted on Motivosity. I think it is a great product to offer staff.
reviews.review_pros: I like to thank my immediate employees for the contributions they provide the branch. I believe that most like to be recognized in some form or another and Moivosity is the place to post.
reviews.review_cons: Not enough Motivosity Bucks to share with other team members. However I really enjoy logging in and seeing other team members send appreations to their immediate staff or to staff located at other branches.

reviews.review_title: Great feature presented in a simple to use way
reviews.review_description: I love it, looks great, easy to use, and easy to integrate with other apps, like Slack. We love using it to communicate each others achievements.
reviews.review_pros: I love that it's simple and entertaining to use! Motivosity represents a simple feature in a not overcomplicated shell. Love that you can recognise more than one person at the time, an entire team, and not only with money, because that's not all that matters. I love the public and private mode too!
reviews.review_cons: Recognition categories are not always applicable, but luckily you don't have to use them every time.

reviews.review_description: Motivosity seems like a great tool for organizations wanting to build a more connected and appreciative work environment. However, businesses should weigh the cost against the value it adds, especially in terms of customization and support.
reviews.review_pros: The ability to recognize employees' achievements through peer-to-peer recognition. This builds a positive work culture and promotes a sense of appreciation among team members.
reviews.review_cons: There’s a concern that over-reliance on recognition systems like Motivosity could lead to a "trophy culture", where employees are more focused on receiving recognition for every small task, rather than doing the hard work that drives meaningful outcomes. This can dilute the impact of genuine recognition, making it feel less authentic or meaningful over time.

reviews.review_title: Still one of the best Kudos Systems
reviews.review_pros: I love the ability to thank my co-workers for working over the top and also use a variety of emojis/GIFs/text to convey my genuine message.
reviews.review_cons: The GIF options do seem somewhat limited, but thats otherwise fine. Beyond that, theres only some nitpicks about how the site loads when logging back in. Seems like it takes a couple tries at times

reviews.review_description: It has been a great tool for employee engagement and recognition, and it has become a big part of our company culture. It's also been a great way to reward fellow coworkers and employees.
reviews.review_pros: It's easy to use and acts as a social media platform for the company, which is more secure and gives team members a chance to interact more with each other outside of their main workflow.
reviews.review_cons: I'd like to see a few more options for ways employees can interact with each other using the platform, outside of sending appreciations.

reviews.review_description: My company implemented Motivosity a few years ago and our morale has improved so much throughout the company. We see our employees fully engaging with the site and really showing their appreciation for each other. We love Motivosity!
reviews.review_pros: That I can shoutout people without giving money. This increases the likelihood that others will add on money, or that we can just give our appreciation to someone without the inclusion of funds.
reviews.review_cons: I have an error where I keep receiving the same pop up from a kudos I received 2 years ago. I wish that glitch could be fixed.

reviews.review_description: Very user friendly and easy to set up and use daily. No drawbacks and all plusses as far as we know.
reviews.review_pros: The custom ways employees can recognize their peers. I really appreciate how quickly employees can use it.
reviews.review_cons: I didn't find any drawbacks to using it and our employees used it natively almost right off the bat.

reviews.review_description: Motivosity has been a great addition to our workplace. It’s easy to use, encourages positivity, and helps reinforce a culture of appreciation. While there’s room for improvement in analytics and customization, the overall impact has been very positive.
reviews.review_pros: Motivosity makes it incredibly easy to recognize and appreciate team members in real time. The social-style feed keeps engagement high, and I love how recognition can be tied to company values. It’s intuitive, fun, and genuinely helps build a more connected and positive workplace culture.
reviews.review_cons: The reporting features could be more robust—especially when trying to analyze long-term engagement trends. Also, the interface can feel a bit overwhelming for new users, and more customization options for notifications would be helpful.

reviews.review_description: My experience with Motivosity is really positive. It makes recognition and appreciation feel simple and genuine, which helps boost team morale. The platform is easy to use, encourages more peer-to-peer recognition, and creates a stronger sense of connection and engagement across the team
reviews.review_pros: I love how Motivosity makes it easy to recognize and appreciate teammates and other team members can see the great work / team work is happening across the mkt function
reviews.review_cons: It would be awesome if the dollars not spent could roll over into the next month. because sometimes there aren’t as many opportunities to give during a busy period. Having the unused balance carry over would give more flexibility and encourage people to use their funds more meaningfully when the right moment comes

reviews.review_description: Motivosity is a wonderful platform for any company! I had never heard of it until I started working at Becklar, and I think all companies should use it for the purpose of bringing employees together and building rapport.
reviews.review_pros: I like that Motivosity unites employees!
reviews.review_cons: I personally cannot think of anything that I dislike about Motivosity, it is very easy to navigate and shoutout our peers/coworkers!

reviews.review_description: We have been using Motivosity for several years and really like the overall experience. The support is fantastic and it is so easy to provide a nice benefit to the company. There are also some nice and easy ways to cash in on the benefits provided to employees.
reviews.review_pros: We love helping our employees connect and recognize their accomplishments. It's so easy to reward them for their accomplishments and get them excited for events and activities.
reviews.review_cons: We have found that we often get stuck in a rut and forget to engage in the platform. Many employees reward the same people month over month.

reviews.review_description: I enjoy knowing that anything I come across will be positive, the application itself is easy to maneuver and to add new employees is easy as well.
reviews.review_pros: The positive engagement with the staff. I like that it is easy to create and give awards to staff for anything they do.
reviews.review_cons: Nothing stands out at this time. I am excited to see the new store platform that I was visited about with my meeting with Jordan.

reviews.review_title: Great Way To Implement Team Recognition
reviews.review_description: I think Motivosity has been a great tool for our company so we can give each other recognition and motivates people with rewards.
reviews.review_pros: I love that Motivosity it helps promote team recognition and offers rewards. It really helps us appreciate one another.
reviews.review_cons: I love everything about Motivosity but if there was something I had to pick I would say the email notifications.

reviews.review_description: It is an amazing platform that I would recommended to all companies! I love that you can learn about each other, add gifs, send/create awards and badges!
reviews.review_pros: I love how user friendly it is! I also love how much of a positive impact it has had on our company culture.
reviews.review_cons: Nothing really. It would be nice to see previous birthdays/milestones for a few days in the right side of the page. Sometimes I feel like I may miss them if I’m not checking everyday.

reviews.review_title: Motivosity: A Fun and Engaging Tool for Employee Recognition
reviews.author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
reviews.review_description: My overall experience with Motivosity has been very positive. As a people leader, it has been an invaluable tool for rewarding and recognizing employees in a way that’s fun, uplifting, and incredibly engaging for the entire team
reviews.review_pros: What I love most about Motivosity is how it helps me stay connected with people I might not collaborate directly with but who have a significant impact on how I run my operations. It bridges gaps and ensures recognition and collaboration flow seamlessly across the team.
reviews.review_cons: What I like least about Motivosity is the lack of a built-in direct messaging feature. It would be great to have a way to stay in touch with someone directly within the platform, without needing to switch to GChat or SMS for those conversations.

reviews.author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
reviews.review_description: I would recommend Motivosity to anyone looking for a tangible effective way to motivate, and provide positive reinforcement to your staff and colleagues
reviews.review_pros: Motivosity makes it easy to recognize your colleagues efforts with very little effort. It makes it easy to recognize that what we do impacts not only our patients but our colleagues.
reviews.review_cons: The implementation was a bit rocky for us. Our specialist was not as versed in the product as I would have expected.

reviews.review_title: Great tool to help make your coworkers feel recognized!
reviews.review_description: great experience, it has helped our team have constant and consistent recognition. it makes it easy for anyone to recognize each other.
reviews.review_pros: how easy it is to use! I love reading through everyone's comments. It has given our team a convenient way to give the team recognition in front of their peers.
reviews.review_cons: I wish you got emails when someone comments on your post. I miss them sometimes and feel bad that I didn't see them.
